title: apium trifoliatum
intro: Apium trifoliatum, also known as the Bitter Celery-leaved Parsley, is a plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic. The health benefits of Apium trifoliatum include relief from indigestion, bloating, and urinary issues, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Apium trifoliatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
